An Exciting New Chapter
{{companyName}} is Now Employee-Owned
Company
{{companyName}}
Effective Date

{{ref}}
Instructions: This template is a starting point. Customize the tone, details, and specific language to match your company's culture. Remove this instruction block before distributing to employees.
§ 01The Big News

Dear {{companyName}} Team,

I have some exciting news to share with you. As of {{closingDate}}, {{companyName}} is an employee-owned company.

What does that mean? It means that you — every full-time employee — now have a beneficial ownership stake in the company through an Employee Stock Ownership Plan, or ESOP. This isn't a purchase you need to make. It's a benefit the company provides to you, at no cost to you.

§ 02What Is an ESOP?

An ESOP is a retirement plan that holds stock in the company on behalf of employees. Here's how it works in plain language:

✓You don't pay anything. The ESOP is funded by the company. There is no cost to you, no payroll deduction, and no purchase required.
✓Your account grows over time. As the company pays down its ESOP loan and grows in value, shares are allocated to your account. The more successful the company, the more your account is worth.
✓It's a retirement benefit. When you leave the company — whether through retirement or otherwise — the company buys back your shares at fair market value. You receive the proceeds, which can be rolled into an IRA or taken as cash.
✓You become an owner. While the ESOP trust holds the stock legally, you are the beneficial owner. Your interests are aligned with the company's success.
§ 03What This Means for You

Here's what changes — and what doesn't:

A.Your job is secure. This transition was designed to preserve the company and its jobs. Your role, pay, and benefits are not affected by this change.
B.Management stays in place. The same leadership team that runs the company today will continue to run it. The ESOP is a ownership structure, not a management change.
C.You'll receive statements. Each year, you'll receive a statement showing the value of your ESOP account. These statements are confidential and personal to you.
D.Tax benefits. Employer contributions to the ESOP are tax-deductible for the company, which helps fund growth and employee benefits. Some employees may also qualify for additional tax advantages.
§ 04What Stays the Same

Almost everything about how you work stays the same:

•Your supervisor and reporting structure
•Your pay, benefits, and PTO
•Your daily responsibilities
•The company's name, location, and operations
•Our commitment to our customers and community
§ 05Questions?

We know you'll have questions. That's expected and encouraged. Here's how to get answers:

1.All-hands meeting. We will hold a company-wide meeting on {{allHandsDate}} at {{allHandsTime}} to discuss this transition and answer your questions in person.
2.FAQ document. A plain-language FAQ is attached to this announcement. It covers the most common questions about ESOPs.
3.Confidential questions. If you have personal questions about your specific account or financial situation, you can contact the ESOP administrator at {{esopAdminEmail}}.

This is a proud moment for {{companyName}}. We built this business together, and now we own it together. Thank you for being part of this journey.

—{{companyName}}
{{senderName}}
{{senderTitle}}
